title: Skill Evals
description: Schema for the optional evals.json in a skill folder. Declares runnable test cases used by the eval harness (scripts/run_evals.py) to verify — via an LLM judge — that the skill still produces the expected behavior against the current model.

skill_name.description: Must equal the skill's id (and its folder name).

id.description: Unique identifier within this file (number or short slug).

prompt.description: The user message sent to the skill (SKILL.md body as system prompt). Must be self-contained enough to run without external fixture files unless `files` is set.

expected_output.description: Prose description of what a good response looks like. Given to the judge as grading context.

files.description: Attachments the eval needs. Entries wrapped in <angle brackets> are placeholder descriptions (no real file provided) — the harness marks such evals 'skipped: needs fixture' rather than running them. Real relative paths (resolved against the skill folder) are attached when present.

assertions.description: What must be true of the response. Each is graded pass/fail by the judge model.

text.description: A single, checkable claim about the response.
